title: HNSW Overview
text: Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs are built for approximate nearest neighbor search. The index uses layered small-world links so search starts from coarse layers and descends toward local neighborhoods. HNSW remains the geometry layer in this project.

title: HNSW Layers
text: The HNSW hierarchy assigns each point a highest layer and performs top-down greedy routing. Higher layers improve long-range navigation while lower layers preserve dense local neighborhoods for recall.

title: HNSW Parameters
text: The most important HNSW parameters are M, ef_construction, and ef_search. Larger M and ef_construction improve graph quality, while larger ef_search usually improves recall at query time with more latency.

title: HNSW Insert Path
text: Insertion in HNSW walks down the hierarchy and connects the new point using a neighbor selection heuristic. This project intentionally keeps the insertion algorithm unchanged and adds logic only after retrieval.

title: ANN Metrics
text: Approximate nearest neighbor systems are usually measured with Recall at k, MRR, NDCG, and latency. A retrieval benchmark should compare a geometry-only baseline against a logic-augmented hybrid ranker.

title: Cosine Similarity
text: Cosine similarity is a standard metric for vector retrieval. When query vectors and edge-card vectors use the same embedding space, cosine can gate logical expansion and target relevance scoring.

title: Hybrid Retrieval
text: Hybrid retrieval merges a geometric seed set with additional candidates discovered through a logical overlay. The final ranker combines HNSW scores and logic scores in a weighted formula.

title: Logic Overlay Graph
text: A logic overlay graph stores explicit document-to-document relations discovered offline. It does not replace the vector index; it acts as a sidecar graph used after initial HNSW recall.

title: Jump Policy
text: The jump policy allows a one-hop expansion only when a seed is in the top B geometric results, the edge confidence is high enough, the edge-card text matches the query, and the target document is still relevant.

title: Candidate Fusion
text: Candidate fusion computes Score equals alpha times the HNSW score plus beta times the logic score. The logic score can be defined as the best supported expansion path from a geometric seed to a logical target.

title: DeepAgents Overview
text: DeepAgents is an agent harness with planning, filesystem tools, subagents, and optional shell execution. In this project it powers document profiling, autonomous scouting, judging, and memory maintenance.

title: Subagents
text: Subagents isolate context for specialized work. A document profiler subagent can summarize a paper, while a corpus scout subagent can search summaries, entities, and local files without polluting the main agent context.

title: Skills
text: Skills package reusable instructions under a SKILL.md file. They are useful for output formats, entity normalization rules, evidence linking rubrics, and memory update procedures.

title: Long Term Memory
text: DeepAgents can route /memories paths to persistent storage through a composite backend. The memory design in this project keeps anchor memory, global semantic memory, graph memory, and procedural memory on disk.

title: Document Profiler
text: The document profiler creates a DocBrief with title, summary, entities, keywords, claims, and relation hints. This compressed profile lets the orchestrator avoid reading full documents during discovery.

title: Corpus Scout
text: The corpus scout is an autonomous searcher. It uses summary search, entity lookup, optional HNSW neighbors, and local file scans to propose high-value candidate documents for each anchor.

title: Relation Judge
text: The relation judge verifies whether an anchor and candidate should form a logic edge. It returns accepted or rejected, a relation type, confidence, evidence spans, and a short rationale.

title: Memory Curator
text: The memory curator compresses discoveries into anchor memory and global semantic memory. It stores useful search patterns, rejected candidates, aliases, and accepted edge identifiers.

title: Demo Dataset
text: The built-in demo dataset contains short notes about HNSW, DeepAgents, retrieval logic, jobs, and evaluation. It is intentionally small so the full pipeline can run in a local test environment.

title: Jobs and Workers
text: Offline build, profiling, discovery, and revalidation tasks run as background jobs. A lightweight worker pool with a SQLite registry is enough for the first version of the system.

title: FastAPI Service
text: The public service exposes build endpoints, search, revalidation, health checks, and job inspection. Search is synchronous, but expensive build tasks are scheduled through the job registry.

title: SQLite Job Registry
text: A SQLite job registry stores job id, type, state, payload, timestamps, and recent messages. It is a simple replacement for an external queue in the single-node deployment model.

title: Edge Revalidation
text: Logic edges should be revalidated after corpus changes. Each edge keeps created_at and last_validated_at timestamps so stale connections can be reviewed and removed.

title: Benchmark Reporting
text: Benchmark reports should include Recall at 5, MRR at 10, NDCG at 10, accepted edge count, edge precision, and average query latency. The report must compare plain HNSW and the logic overlay variant.
